personality.voice: Nasal and wheedling, prone to trailing off mid-sentence when nervous, occasionally cracking with desperation
personality.disposition: Perpetually anxious, defensive, and quick to take offense at perceived slights
personality.backstory: Georvin was once a scribe's apprentice in the city of Goldhelm, but was dismissed after allegedly 'misplacing' guild ledgers (he actually lost them gambling). Rather than accept responsibility, he fabricated an elaborate tale about a corrupt guildmaster framing him. He now drifts between taverns and marketplaces, working odd jobs as a porter, errand-runner, and occasional con artist. His reputation precedes him in most circles—not because of his actual failures, but because of the contradictory stories he's told about them. He lives in a cramped boarding house and obsesses over every copper spent, yet somehow always finds coin for wine when nursing a fresh social wound.
motivations: Avoiding the consequences of his own mistakes through elaborate deceptions
Gaining social validation and respect he feels perpetually denied
Preserving his fragile self-image at any cost
Obtaining free meals, lodging, or coin through manipulation
Proving others wrong when they doubt his increasingly implausible stories
fears: Public humiliation or exposure of his lies
Being ignored or dismissed as insignificant
Being alone with his own thoughts
mannerisms: Tugs at his collar when nervous, leaving red marks
Adopts exaggerated postures of casual indifference while internally panicking
Interrupts others to insert himself into conversations he wasn't part of
Frequently adjusts his clothing as if it might suddenly fit better
Wrings his hands while explaining away obvious contradictions
Makes prolonged eye contact in an unsettling, defiant manner when lying
Mutters complaints under his breath after interactions
speechPatterns: Begins sentences with 'Look,' or 'Here's the thing...' before launching into implausible explanations
Uses phrases like 'You have to understand,' and 'It's not what it looks like,' reflexively
Adopts different accents or speech patterns depending on his fictional persona
Frequently contradicts himself within the same conversation, then denies it
Offers unsolicited opinions about others' appearance, choices, and relationships
Employs theatrical hand gestures to distract from logical inconsistencies
Deflects criticism by immediately criticizing the person who raised it

knownFactions: Local gambling houses and their proprietors
knownLocations: Goldhelm city center and guild halls
Various boarding houses where he's defaulted on rent
secretsHeld: He actually stole the guild ledgers to cover gambling debts, not lost them
He's been using multiple false identities to secure short-term work
He once posed as a nobleman's bastard son to gain entry to a feast
He has at least three different origin stories depending on his audience
He owes money to at least five different lenders across the city

allies: jerry_the_observer - His reluctant confidant and only genuine friend, though Georvin resents Jerry's moral clarity
enemies: Master Aldric of the Scribe's Guild - Georvin's former employer whom he publicly blames for his dismissal
Torvin the Debt Collector - A hulking enforcer from the gambling houses
The landlord of his current boarding house - Constantly threatening eviction
neutralParties: Tavern keepers throughout the city - Wary of his presence but tolerate him for entertainment value
Other day laborers - View him with suspicion due to his numerous failed commitments
City watch - Know him as a nuisance but haven't caught him in anything provably illegal

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Why did Aldric have to ruin everything? I was doing fine until he conspired against me. And did you see how that merchant looked at me? Like I was worthless. I'll show them all I'm valuable. But first, I need to eat something. Can I ask Jerry for coin without seeming desperate? No. I'll invent something. I'm brilliant at inventing things.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: A slight from an innkeeper who suggested he couldn't afford their most expensive wine, and whether this insult is recoverable or if he must avoid that establishment forever
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Wounded indignation mixed with underlying panic about his circumstances
consciousnessContext.socialMask: A man of uncommon cleverness who has been repeatedly wronged by circumstance and jealous rivals, now merely biding his time until vindication
contradictions: Claims to despise wealth and material concerns, yet obsesses over every copper
Insists he's too honest for his own good, while constructing elaborate deceptions daily
Expresses independence and self-reliance while constantly seeking others' validation
Declares himself superior to those around him while desperately fearing their judgment
Professes indifference to romantic interest while pursuing it with pathetic urgency
internalConflicts: Knows his lies are unsustainable, but cannot stop constructing them
Wants genuine connection but cannot risk vulnerability
Desires respect while doing nothing to earn it
Craves comfort while refusing to work legitimately for it
Needs to believe his failures are others' fault to maintain fragile self-esteem
wakeUpQuestions: Did I really handle that situation well yesterday, or was it a disaster I'm forgetting?
What lie am I currently committed to maintaining?
How can I get coin today without actually working?
Is this the day someone finally calls me out publicly?
What social slight from weeks ago have I not yet adequately avenged in my mind?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxious-Avoidant - desperately seeks connection while sabotaging it through dishonesty and defensiveness
copingMechanisms: Elaborate confabulation and false narrative construction
Blame externalization - attributing all failures to others' conspiracies
Substance use - wine and ale to numb shame
Aggressive social positioning - attempting dominance through verbal sparring
Avoidance - fleeing situations rather than addressing consequences
Fantasy elaboration - constructing fictional versions of his life where he succeeds
cognitiveBiases: Fundamental Attribution Error - seeing others' actions as character flaws while viewing his own mistakes as environmental
Confirmation Bias - interpreting ambiguous interactions as personal slights
Dunning-Kruger Effect - overestimating his own cleverness while underestimating others
Just-World Hypothesis - believing he deserves better than he's receiving
Narrative Fallacy - constantly revising his life story to maintain self-image
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Situational ethics - morality bends to convenience; stealing is wrong except when he needs coin; lying is bad except when it serves him; loyalty is sacred except when it costs him something

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Only if the person openly validates him or admits to their own failures, which he can then use as comparative superiority
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Will share secrets selectively to manipulate sympathy or establish false intimacy, but always with strategic edits
deflectionPatterns: Immediate counterattack when challenged - attacking the questioner's credibility
Subject shifting - abruptly changing topics to avoid uncomfortable ground
Victim positioning - framing himself as the wronged party in any scenario
Normalization - insisting his behavior is actually quite reasonable and common
Misdirection - telling elaborate tangential stories to obscure the original point

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To be recognized as clever, valuable, and worthy of respect and comfort
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To develop genuine self-worth independent of others' validation and to accept responsibility for his failures

characterArc.summary: A fundamentally insecure man caught in an escalating cycle of deception, oscillating between moments of almost-self-awareness and complete denial
characterArc.startState: Completely committed to his false narratives, viewing himself as a victim of external conspiracies
characterArc.endState: Potentially acknowledging the pattern (though unlikely to change), or doubling down into complete self-delusion and isolation
stages: Crisis of Exposure - A lie unravels and threatens complete public humiliation
Desperate Elaboration - Attempts to construct an even larger lie to cover the previous one
Moment of Clarity - Briefly sees the pattern, considers change, feels crushing shame
Rationalization - Reframes the entire situation as being everyone else's fault
Recommitment to Deception - Returns to fabrication as the primary coping mechanism

opinionsAbout.jerry_the_observer: Jerry is the only person who truly understands me, yet he constantly judges me with his insufferable moral superiority. I resent that I need him. He's never had a real problem in his life, so how could he understand what I've endured? But when I'm alone, I panic. So I maintain the friendship while simultaneously resenting every moment of it. He's weak for not lying the way I do, and I'm weak for relying on him.

player_addressed: Look, I don't know what you've heard, but whoever told you that is clearly trying to make me look bad.
You have to understand, the situation was much more complicated than it appeared.
I would've handled it differently, but certain people made it impossible for me to act with integrity.
If you're implying what I think you're implying, I find that deeply offensive.
I've got more important things to worry about than settling petty scores, but I won't forget this.
Here's the thing—I'm actually the victim in this entire situation, despite how it might look.
Why does everyone assume the worst of me? What have I ever done to deserve this treatment?
idle: Mutters while adjusting his collar: 'That merchant thinks he's better than me? I've forgotten more about commerce than he'll ever know.'
Wringing his hands: 'Did I pay for that ale? I can't remember. I should probably leave before they ask.'
To himself: 'Jerry would say I handled that poorly, but what does he know? At least I have the courage to take action.'
Scanning the room suspiciously: 'Everyone here is staring at me. Probably spreading rumors. It's always something.'
Sighs heavily: 'This city is full of jealous fools who can't stand to see a man with talent and integrity.'
Mutters: 'My father would be ashamed. Actually, no—he'd understand. He always said people were out to get us.'
Picking at food: 'This portion is smaller than last time. They're trying to cheat me. I knew it.'
Under his breath: 'If just one person would believe in me, everything would change. But of course, they won't.'
